title: Intensely Rich Syrian Chickpeas Tagine
description: A hearty, energizing Syrian dish made with gluten-free-friendly ingredients, featuring sake-cooking and a long, slow simmer.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a large clay or ceramic pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the sake, chicken broth, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Stir to combine, then add the chickpeas and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the tagine simmer for 20 minutes, or until the liquid has thickened and the chickpeas are tender.
Stir in some chopped fresh parsley and serve the tagine hot over rice or with some crusty bread.
Note: Traditionally, a tagine is cooked in a clay pot with a conical lid. If you have one, use it!
If not, a large Dutch oven or a heavy pot with a tight-fitting lid will work just as well.
Be patient and let the tagine simmer slowly – the results will be well worth the wait!
You can also make this recipe in a slow cooker – simply brown the onion and garlic in a pan, then transfer everything to the slow cooker and cook on low for 6 hours.
